title: calliandra pilocarpa
intro: Calliandra pilocarpa, also known as "powder puff tree," is a flowering plant species native to Central and South America, characterized by its vibrant red, pink, or purple flowers resembling a pom-pom or powder puff. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Calliandra pilocarpa include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as digestive problems and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
